title: Orbit and Attitude Control Engineer
description:
We are building software that tracks what moves in orbit and flags what matters. You will be working with orbit determination in the intersection between orbital mechanics, mission operations, and intelligence.
  
While you will touch upon many of our projects, your primary work will be to lead the technical work on our LSSA-Pilot project, developing the algorithms, data pipelines, and operational framing that make space situational awareness usable for non-engineers. You will work with our flight dynamicsists, who know the orbital mechanics. Your job is to translate sensor data into orbit knowledge and on to Space Situational Awareness. This role grows naturally toward technical leadership.
  
  

WHAT YOU WILL WORK ON
  
Orbit determination, object tracking, and proximity analysis algorithms
  
Integration of external space object catalogs and data sources
  
Conjunction screening, collision avoidance computation, and launch safety analysis
  
**Defining CONOPS-level requirements:** what operational users need to see, when, and why
  
Collaboration with research and industry partners in the SSA domain
  
  

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR
  
Mid-to-senior candidate, 5–8 years of experience. Operational instinct comes from years in mission rooms; we need seniority here.
  
Experience with how real missions are operated — not just the physics, but the process
  
Background in controls engineering, preferably applied to orbital mechanics and flight dynamics
  
Ability to bridge the gap between rigorous analysis and what decision-makers need to act on
  
Strong programming skills in Python
  
Understanding of orbit determination or propagation techniques
  
MSc or PhD in control engineering, aerospace engineering, physics, applied mathematics, or equivalent
  
  

USEFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED
  
Experience with space object catalogs, TLE formats, or conjunction data messages
  
Familiarity with CCSDS standards
  
Interest in defense applications
  
Background in mission control, flight operations, or CONOPS development
  
  

REQUIREMENTS
  
Fluent in English; Swedish is a plus
  
EU or EES citizen
  
  

ABOUT VINTERSTELLAR
  
Vinterstellar is a Stockholm-based aerospace company doing mission analysis and flight dynamics for satellite operators and space agencies — from GEO telecommunications to Earth observation to deep-space trajectory design. Founded in 2018, we are five people and growing, with multiple funded development projects running and a product evolving into a decision-oriented mission analysis platform for the European market.
  
At Vinterstellar you will have real ownership from day one. Together we work hybrid and have a lot of freedom when it comes to structuring our time and the way we want to work.
  
  

HOW TO APPLY
  
  

**Send your CV and a short note about yourself to:**  

**Applications:** careers@vinterstellar.se
  
**Role questions:** Emil Vinterhav, CEO — emil.vinterhav@vinterstellar.se
  
**Process questions:** Andreas Thorell Berchtold — andreas.thorell@vinterstellar.se
  
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. We hire as soon as we find the right person.

title: Technical Intern (Software & AI Support)
description:
### **About Depict**


At Depict.ai, we are fundamentally disrupting how e-commerce operations have been conducted to date. We are building the intelligence engine for the future of retail, leveraging applied AI to transform product discovery, merchandising, and the overall shopper experience. We are not here to build a feature or a quick flip; our ambition is to build a generational, legacy company that redefines the global e-commerce software ecosystem.

**The Role**


We are looking for a hungry, highly motivated Technical Intern to join our engineering team. In this role, you will be the crucial bridge between our technology and our customers. You won’t just be fetching coffee; you will be writing code, solving real-world customer problems, maintaining our software, and actively integrating AI into our operational workflows.


This internship is designed to be a launchpad. For the right candidate, this role has a direct path to a full-time position as a Junior Software Developer or AI Engineer upon successful completion of the internship.

 **Key Responsibilities**

* Customer Support Engineering: Act as the first line of technical defense, investigating, troubleshooting, and resolving technical issues raised by our customers.
* Software Maintenance & Bug Fixing: Dive into our codebase to identify bugs, write patches, and deploy fixes to keep our systems running smoothly.
* AI Implementation: Identify bottlenecks in our customer support and internal workflows, and build AI-driven solutions (e.g., using LLMs or automation tools) to streamline and scale these processes.
* Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with both the core engineering and customer success teams to communicate user feedback and improve our core product.

 **Who You Are**

* Educational Background: You are in your final year of studies or have recently graduated with a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
* Strong Technical Foundation: You have solid coding skills (e.g., Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, etc.) and are comfortable navigating complex codebases and APIs.
* Problem Solver: You enjoy getting to the bottom of technical mysteries and possess strong debugging skills.
* Hungry & Driven: You are highly motivated, eager to learn, and excited by the pace and ambiguity of an early-stage startup. You don't wait for tasks to be handed to you; you take initiative.
* AI Enthusiast: You are passionate about artificial intelligence and eager to apply AI creatively to solve practical, everyday business problems.

 **What We Offer**

* Real Responsibility: Gain hands-on experience building, maintaining, and supporting software that impacts real customers.
* Career Growth: A clear trajectory to a full-time Junior Software Developer or AI Engineer role based on your performance.
* Mentorship: Work side-by-side with experienced engineers and founders who are invested in your learning and growth.
* Startup Culture: A fast-paced, collaborative, and fun environment where your ideas matter from day one.


We look forward to hearing from you, and best of luck!

title: R&D Engineer – Transformer Windings
description:
**Location:**

Ludvika, Dalarna County, Sweden
**Job ID:**

R0100797
**Date Posted:**

2025-10-15
**Company Name:**

HITACHI ENERGY SWEDEN AB
**Profession (Job Category):**

Engineering & Science
**Job Schedule:**

Full time
**Remote:**

No
**Job Description:**

**The opportunity**

For our HVDC Transformers research and development team we are looking for a R&D mechanical engineer in the field of Transformer Winding technology. You will lead activities to improve transformer winding performance, design and manufacturing methods while challenging legacy routines and developing better suited concepts for the future. The role will cooperate closely with experts in different transformer technology fields as well as with transformer design and manufacturing specialists globally. You will build and maintain a network with the HVDC Transformer factories globally, collecting the inputs and supporting implementation of new development.


Working in R&D will give you the possibility to use your creativity to solve a wide spectrum of challenges. You will have exciting and varied work in front of you where you will get a broad network internally and externally. Work with both the local factory and the global organization within Transformers and have career development opportunities as a Specialist or as a Project Manager.


**How you'll make an impact**

* Creating conceptual designs of Transformer windings to meet specified requirements (functional, technical, quality, etc).
* Ability to create detailed design and 3D modeling as well as running mechanical verifications using simulation tools.
* Running Design for Excellence practices (DFA, DFM, FMEA, etc.).
* Preparing reports and related documents in accordance with internal and external requirements.
* Technical standards: Ensure that winding-related processes and calculation methods are state of the art. Work with programmers to implement and test new methods in the design software.
* Technical support: Support the design, manufacturing, quality and supply chain organizations with technical expertise to ensure that technical requirements are fulfilled.
* Technical leadership and knowledge sharing: Coach and provide support and technical advice to colleagues on winding-related topics. Maintain a network of engineering professionals to share best practices across different disciplines and the HVDC Transformer factories.

**Your background**

* Master’s or Bachelor’s degree combined with work-life experience in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent.
* 3+ Years of experience in Design & Engineering.
* Domain expertise in Power transformers.
* Fundamental knowledge of using different materials and manufacturing methods.
* Understanding of electrical, magnetic and thermal phenomena.
* Advanced knowledge of 3D CAD software Creo/ProE.
* Fluent command of English, both written and spoken.
* Willingness to learn and try new things without fear of failure!

**What we offer**

* Collective agreement
* Flexible working time
* Health care and wellness allowance
* Fantastic career possibilities within Hitachi Energy both within Sweden and globally
* Mentor to support you throughout onboard phase
* Various trainings and education supporting employee development
* Diversified company with over 70+ nationalities working in Sweden
* Supplementary compensation for parental leave
* Employee Benefit Portal with thousands of discounts and perks

**More about us**

Are you ready for an exciting new challenge? Does the above description sound like you? Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don’t delay – apply today!


Recruiting Manager Karolina Czechowski, karolina.czechowski@hitachienergy.com , will answer your questions about the position. title: Software Engineer - Product
description:
At Endra, our vision is to produce software of exceptional quality. Attaining this standard requires both talent and ownership. We seek individuals who care deeply about the quality of the software we deliver, whether their focus is engineering, design, support, or sales.
   

  

 Join our small and talented team as the technical link between our platform and our users. You’ll own features from concept to deployment, working directly with customers to shape the future of MEP design. We’re looking for engineers who love solving real-world problems, care about user experience, and are driven to see their code make an immediate impact.
   

  

**What We’re Looking For**
* 5+ years shipping real product work in C++, TypeScript or Python, or building modern front‑end apps, so you can hit the ground running and ship features customers actually use.
* Confident in taking ownership of front-end features from start to finish, working collaboratively with backend services, and debugging across the stack.
* You enjoy talking with users, turning feedback into product improvements, and seeing your work solve real problems in the field.
* Experience with CAD/BIM or a prior product‑company background.


**What You'll Do**
* Write, review, and maintain clean, well-tested code across the stack; drive best practices for code quality, performance, security, and observability.
* Improve frontend architecture, component libraries, state management, build pipelines, and performance at scale.
* Manage and maintain databases, APIs, and other backend services, ensuring reliability, performance, and security.
* Design and implement flawless UX to enable end users to create quickly and efficiently.
* Serve as the technical lead for customer engagements, translating user feedback and pain points into product features and bug fixes.
* Communicate technical trade-offs and progress clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders in English and Swedish.


**Our current tech stack**
* Core Logic (C++23): A high-performance, modern C++23 codebase developed from scratch. It underpins our system with geometric primitives, advanced optimization algorithms, and the core business logic essential for our MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing) decision-making and validation frameworks.
* Backend Services (Python/GCP): A lightweight asynchronous Python API deployed on GCP. It manages database interactions (Postgres), generates required reports, and interfaces with our high-speed C++ Core library.
* Frontend Interface (Svelte/TypeScript/WebAssembly): A compact, high-performance CAD application delivered directly in the user's browser. It is built using Svelte, extensive custom TypeScript, and WebAssembly components, ensuring a minimal, clean, and highly responsive user experience.


**How We Operate**
* Less is more: We simplify and ship smaller, fast.
* Build the right things: We listen carefully and build what users actually need.
* Velocity & Quality: We build things that last, but we don’t perfect them forever.
* Trust & Collaboration: Strong opinions, loosely held.


**What we offer**
* Competitive salary and equity.
* Employee-friendly equity terms.
* Private health insurance.
* Regular team events and off-sites.
* Six weeks of paid vacation.
* An inspiring, ambitious, and global work environment.


**Ready to move the needle?**
 Do you feel this is your opportunity to do your most meaningful work and reshape an entire industry? Let's talk!

title: Software Engineer & IT Operations Lead
description:
Are you a skilled Software Engineer who enjoys building new solutions, modernising existing systems, and serving as the natural go-to person for all things IT? Do you want to help shape a modern IT environment in an international company where technology actively enables the business?
 



  





 We are now looking for a Software
 
 Engineer
 
 with broad IT responsibilities to join JCDecaux Sverige AB in Stockholm.
 



  





**About the company** 




 JCDecaux is a global group present in 4,400 cities across 89 countries. Our advertising messages reach more than 340 million people every day. In total, approximately 11,900 people work at JCDecaux, and the head office is located just outside Paris in Neuilly-sur-Seine.
 



  





**About the role** 




 In this role, you will be a key member of a small, dynamic IT organisation where you both drive development initiatives and ensure a stable, efficient day-to-day operation. You will work closely with the IT Director and experienced colleagues and over time you will become one of the people with the best overview of our system landscape.
 



  





 In short: you are the one who keeps the light on and turns plans into reality – both in the codebase and in the underlying infrastructure.
 



  





**Examples of responsibilities and tasks:** 




 ·    Drive our IT environment forward by modernising, stabilising and automating existing solutions.
 



 ·    Develop IT solutions for our core systems, primarily on the Microsoft platform (.NET, SQL Server, Azure).
 



 ·    Act as a natural point of contact for IT-related questions internally (first line support for employees).
 



 ·    Serve as a link to external vendors, sister offices in other countries and the group IT function, the group IT function and key customers.
 



 ·    Implement and improve processes for onboarding/offboarding, licence management, user administration and access control.
 



 ·    Contribute to requirements work together with the business – capture needs, translate them into technical solutions and follow them through to production.
 



 ·    Maintain and develop our IT/system map and work towards increased standardisation and automation.
 



 ·    Participate in and occasionally lead agile ways of working (Scrum/Kanban) together with developers, product owners and other key stakeholders.
 



  





 Technology environment (examples)
 



 ·    .NET / C# (occasionally VB.NET).
 



 ·    MS SQL Server and Oracle.
 



 ·    MS Azure and on-premise solutions.
 



 ·    REST/JSON-based integrations.
 



  





 Over time, you will play a key role in shaping the tools, platforms, and ways of working that will define the future of IT at JCDecaux Sweden.
 



  





**Who you are** 




 We believe you recognise yourself in many of the following:
 



 ·    You have at least 5 years of experience in system development in .NET (C#) and thrive in backend-oriented roles.
 



 ·    You have strong skills in .NET, C#, MS SQL and modern APIs (REST/JSON).
 



 ·    You are comfortable working in Microsoft environments in general (servers, networks, cloud services, preferably Azure).
 



 ·    You have experience from agile environments (Scrum/Kanban) and enjoy working closely with business stakeholders.
 



 ·    You appreciate both understanding business requirements and proposing robust technical solutions.
 



 ·    You are curious, unpretentious and enjoy sharing knowledge with others.
 



 ·    You take strong ownership, value autonomy and enjoy being “the spider in the web” in a smaller IT organisation.
 



  





 Fluency in both spoken and written English is required. Knowledge of French is considered an additional advantage.
 



 Nice to have (not mandatory)
 



 ·    Experience of system or solution architecture.
 



 ·    Experience of Microsoft Dynamics 365.
 



 ·    Experience of Power BI, Oracle, IoT or web development (e.g. .NET Core, modern frontend frameworks).
 



 ·    Experience acting as a bridge between technology and business, for example, in the role of technical project manager or team lead.
 



  





**Personal qualities** 




 ·    A strong problem-solver who gets energy from simplifying, automating and improving ways of working.
 



 ·    You are comfortable working with both modern technologies and legacy systems and appreciate the importance of incremental change.
 



 ·    You have an entrepreneurial mindset and are comfortable taking initiative.
 



 ·    You are communicative and confident in dialogue with developers, managers, suppliers and other stakeholders
 



  





 Why JCDecaux Sverige AB
 



 ·    A high level of influence – you join early in a journey where the IT environment is being modernised and built for the future.
 



 ·    A role where the combination of development, architecture, operations and support makes your workday varied and stimulating.
 



 ·    Close collaboration with a modern, coaching manager and colleagues who are driven and forward-looking.
 



 ·    An international context with companies in many countries and a head office in France.
 



 ·    A modern office in central Stockholm, shared breakfast on Fridays, recurring conferences and more.
 



  





**Practical information** 




 ·    Location: Stockholm, office at Stureplan.
 



 ·    Type of employment: Permanent employment.
 



 ·    Scope: Full-time.
 



 ·    Salary and benefits: Competitive fixed salary and pension.
 



  





**Application and contact** 




 If you have any questions about the position, you are welcome to contact:
 



  
 



  Jenni Trennestam
 



  Email: jenni.trennestam@jcdecaux.com
 



  Phone: +46 (0)70 690 82 48
 



  





  
 



  We review applications on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible.
